name: Gemini 3.1 Pro

price.provider: Google AI Studio / Vertex AI (Gemini API)

note: Coding-arena leader with extended long-context frontier reasoning; ARC-AGI-2 77.1%, 1M-token context.

results.notes: ARC-AGI-2, verified score, more than double Gemini 3 Pro
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools (51.4% with search+code execution). Confirmed on both deepmind.google/models/gemini/pro/ and deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-1-pro/.
GDPval-AA, Elo-style rating for expert/professional tasks.
tau2-bench Telecom domain (labeled 'τ2-bench Telecom' on the page). Page also shows a separate 'τ2-bench Retail' score of 90.8%, which has no matching taxonomy slug and was left out.
BrowseComp agentic web-browsing benchmark, Search+Python+Browse tool setting.
Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Gemini CLI harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 15.8% / Score 32.0% (rank 34). Score component used per corpus convention.
MMMLU (Multilingual MMLU), single attempt. Confirmed via direct fetch of the official model page; sits in the same performance table as the 12 benchmarks already tracked but was not yet captured.

τ2-bench Retail domain (labeled 'τ2-bench Retail' on the page, alongside the tracked τ2-bench Telecom score). [taxonomy-gap backfill: this score was already verified in an earlier research round but had no matching benchmark slug at the time; added once the slug was introduced.]
Score extracted from the page's embedded data-target HTML attribute since a plain static fetch only shows client-side-animated placeholder values.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via blog.google (2026-02-19 launch) and deepmind.google/models/gemini/pro/ (fetched directly). All 5 benchmark numbers (GPQA 94.3, ARC-AGI-2 77.1, SWE-bench 80.6, MMMU-Pro 80.5, LiveCodeBench Pro 2887 Elo) literally confirmed on the cited pages. 1M context and $2/$12 pricing confirmed via independent sources. Still in preview as of July 2026 (not yet GA) per multiple July-2026 press coverage — status left as preview, correctly. No changes to record.

[2026-07-16] Researched the six missing core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu). Checked Google's official Feb-2026 model card (deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-1-pro/) and its companion evals-methodology PDF (storage.googleapis.com/deepmind-media/gemini/gemini_3-1_pro_model_evaluation.pdf, downloaded and text-extracted directly), plus Vals AI's independent per-benchmark leaderboard (vals.ai/models/google_gemini-3.1-pro-preview, fetched as raw HTML to read the embedded data-target score attributes since the static page otherwise renders placeholder 0.0% values). Google's official table covers a fixed set of ~16 benchmarks (HLE, ARC-AGI-2, GPQA Diamond, Terminal-Bench 2.0, SWE-Bench Verified/Pro, LiveCodeBench Pro, SciCode, APEX-Agents, GDPval-AA, tau2-bench Retail/Telecom, MCP Atlas, BrowseComp, MMMU-Pro, MMMLU, MRCR v2 128k/1M) and never mentions MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval by name anywhere in either the model card or the eval methodology doc -- these have been superseded in Google's reporting by MMMLU, LiveCodeBench Pro, and SWE-Bench respectively. Notably even AIME, which sibling model Gemini 3 Pro's model card does report (95.0%), is absent from Gemini 3.1 Pro's table, indicating a deliberate reporting-set change rather than an oversight. Vals AI tracks no plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval benchmark for this model at all -- confirmed via the complete href list of every benchmark tile on the model's Vals AI page (only mmlu_pro, gpqa, lcb, mmmu, swebench, terminal-bench-2-1, and a set of domain-specific finance/legal/medical evals are present; no mmlu, math, aime, or humaneval tile exists). All four (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) are therefore left omitted_no_evidence. Added livecodebench 88.48% from Vals AI (rank 2/127) since its 'lcb' tile is confirmed via direct page-title fetch to be plain 'LiveCodeBench' (percentage pass rate), not the Pro/Elo variant already tracked. For mmmu: Vals AI's 'MMMU' tile (href /benchmarks/mmmu) was directly fetched and its own page <title> reads 'MMMU Pro' -- i.e. it actually measures the same MMMU-Pro protocol already captured by this model's existing mmmu-pro entry (80.5, self-reported), not base MMMU. No genuine base-MMMU score was found for this model from any source checked, so mmmu is left omitted_no_evidence rather than double-counting a Pro-protocol result under the wrong slug. (This same vals.ai MMMU/MMMU-Pro mislabeling was found to affect several other files elsewhere in the corpus -- flagged separately for a dedicated cleanup pass, not touched here.)

[2026-07-17] Second-pass deep research on the four legacy-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval), deliberately avoiding the first pass's checklist (official launch page, model card, Epoch AI, Vals AI, LMArena, BenchLM) and instead checking six new source types:

1. Stanford HELM — fetched the actual underlying data files (not just the JS-rendered page) for all four HELM projects: Capabilities (config.js -> release v1.15.0, schema.json model list), Lite (v1.13.0), Classic (v0.4.0), and Instruct (v1.0.0). Gemini 3.1 Pro appears in none of them: Lite's newest Google model is Gemini 2.0 Flash (Experimental, Dec 2024); Classic tops out at ~2023-era models; Instruct has zero Gemini models at all. Capabilities is the only project current enough in principle (last updated 2026) but its own scenario/group list (fetched via GCS: core_scenarios -> gpqa, ifeval, mmlu_pro, omni_math, wildbench) does not include plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval as scenarios for ANY model, so even if Gemini 3.1 Pro were added it wouldn't produce these numbers. HELM is conclusively a dead end for this model.

2. arXiv — queried the arXiv API directly (export.arxiv.org) with phrase+boolean queries pairing "Gemini 3.1 Pro" against MMLU, HumanEval, AIME, and MATH-500. Found and read ~20 genuine hits mentioning the model (e.g. "Perfect score on IPhO 2025 theory by Gemini agent", "Self-Anchoring Calibration Drift..." comparing Claude Sonnet 4.6/Gemini 3.1 Pro/GPT-5.2, "Measuring Black-Box Confidence via Reasoning Trajectories" which evaluates Gemini 3.1 Pro on MedQA-USMLE/GPQA Diamond/MMLU-Pro for a confidence-calibration method rather than reporting a citable leaderboard-style raw accuracy). None of the located papers report a plain MMLU, MATH-500, AIME, or HumanEval accuracy number for this specific model as a baseline.

3. GitHub eval-harness run logs — GitHub code search turned up a genuine independent third-party harness repo, Lore-Hex/trustedrouter-benchmarks (mirrored into Lore-Hex/quill-router's benchmark_scores.json with "source_class": "T" / real methodology notes, e.g. "200-question stride-sampled subset (TIGER-Lab/MMLU-Pro), 10-choice CoT, letter-match; no judge"). Directly listed its results/ directory (230+ files) and confirmed it ran google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview through exactly three panels: ifeval_panel.json, gsm8k_panel.json, and mmlu_pro_panel.json — there is no math500_panel, aime_panel, humaneval_panel, or plain-mmlu_panel file in the repo for any model, so this harness never tested those four benchmarks against this model. Separately found two derivative "aggregator" repos, anadim/llm-benchmark-matrix and gioe/aiq (benchmark_scores.yaml), that DO carry mmlu/math_500/aime_2025/humaneval numbers keyed to gemini-3.1-pro, but every one of those specific rows cites either an already-checked-and-empty page (deepmind.google/models/model-cards/gemini-3-1-pro/, misattributing the MMMLU=92.6 figure to plain MMLU) or SEO content-farm blogs (automatio.ai, digitalapplied.com). Cross-checking the aggregator entries against our own already-confirmed official numbers exposed clear corruption: gioe/aiq's "gemini-3.1-pro-preview" row lists GPQA_Diamond=91.9 and ARC_AGI_2=31.1, both wrong (confirmed official values are 94.3 and 77.1) and matching Gemini 3 Pro's launch-window data instead — i.e. a sibling-model mislabeling, not real 3.1 Pro data. These aggregator numbers were rejected as not credible.

4. Other leaderboards — OpenRouter's /benchmarks and /performance pages render no numeric data on a static fetch (client-side only). LiveBench.ai: pulled the live leaderboard CSV directly from LiveBench's own GitHub repo (LiveBench/livebench.github.io, public/table_2026_06_25.csv) and confirmed "gemini-3.1-pro-preview-high" is a tested row, but LiveBench uses its own non-equivalent task taxonomy (AMPS_Hard, math_comp, olympiad for math; code_completion/code_generation for coding) rather than literal MMLU/MATH-500/AIME/HumanEval, so it cannot be mapped onto these taxonomy slugs. Scale AI SEAL leaderboard: confirmed real SEAL coverage of this model, but only for SWE-bench Pro/Verified, not the four target benchmarks. PapersWithCode: no dedicated leaderboard entries found for this model on the MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval benchmark pages.

5. Non-English sources — ran targeted Chinese, Japanese, and Korean queries. All hits were either localized/mirror SEO sites (gemini-cn.com, gemini-cnblog.com, uravation.com, ai-revolution.co.jp) restating mutually-inconsistent numbers each query (MMLU cited as 90.0%, 91.4%, and 92.6%-conflated-with-MMMLU across three different searches; HumanEval 88.9%; MATH 85.5%) with no stable, checkable citation — the same content-farm pattern seen in English — or one apparently-genuine Chinese Zhihu blog post ("Gemini 3.1 Pro 跑分77，我翻完了 Model Card 觉得没那么简单") that returned HTTP 403 on fetch and whose title indicates it is discussing the already-known ARC-AGI-2 77.1 score, not new MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval data.

6. Sibling models — searched for official comparison tables in rival labs' own launch/system-card materials (OpenAI GPT-5.4, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6, xAI) that might cite Gemini 3.1 Pro's MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval as a comparison baseline. Found none; all "GPT-5.4 vs Claude Opus 4.6 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro" content was again third-party comparison-blog content (mindstudio.ai, digitalapplied.com, evolink.ai, nxcode.io, apiyi.com, promptquorum.com) with numbers that changed between searches (AIME cited as 100%, 95%, and 91.2% across different queries; MATH cited as 95.1%, 98% GSM8K-conflated) — not a genuine official source.

Verdict: checked all 6 new source types (exceeding the required minimum of 3), with primary-source verification wherever the source type had a real underlying dataset (HELM schema.json model/scenario lists, arXiv API results, GitHub repo file trees and actual JSON run-panel contents, LiveBench's own CSV). No credible, independently-citable MMLU, MATH(-500), AIME, or HumanEval score for Gemini 3.1 Pro was found anywhere. All four gaps remain omitted_no_evidence. The one new, confirmed-negative fact worth flagging for a future cleanup pass: at least two public GitHub "benchmark aggregator" repos (anadim/llm-benchmark-matrix, gioe/aiq) contain demonstrably wrong/conflated Gemini-3.1-Pro numbers (GPQA 91.9 vs. true 94.3, ARC-AGI-2 31.1 vs. true 77.1) sourced from SEO blogs — a caution against ever treating GitHub "benchmark matrix" repos as automatically credible without checking their underlying citations.
